# Behavior reference

Living documentation generated from the functional requirements in
`packages/cli/requirements/functional/` and the end-to-end cases in
`packages/cli/test/e2e/cases/`. Every example below is the expected output
asserted by the e2e suite, so a passing `bun run test:cli:e2e` is also proof
this document is accurate.

**55** requirements · **117** acceptance criteria · **67** end-to-end cases.

Each example shows its full input project (the fixture the command ran
against, including any templates and includes) and, for `generate`, the
files it writes — collapsed by default; expand to verify the recorded
output against its inputs.

## Run

### RUN-FR-0001 — Run renders a named template

The CLI renders `.jastr/<template-id>/template.md` when the user runs `jastr run <template-ref>` from inside a project.

| Criterion | Statement | Coverage |
| --- | --- | --- |
| AC-0001 | Exits with code 0 when the template exists and inputs are valid. | ✅ `basic-run` |
| AC-0002 | Prints rendered Markdown to stdout. | ✅ `basic-run` |
| AC-0003 | Prints nothing to stderr. | ✅ `basic-run` |

#### Case: Run a basic skill

Description: Render a minimal template with no inputs.

Covers: AC-0001, AC-0002, AC-0003

<details>
<summary>Input, command & output</summary>

**Input project** — ran from the project root

```text
./
└─ .jastr/
   └─ demo/
      └─ template.md
```

`.jastr/demo/template.md`

```md
---
name: demo
description: Demo skill
---
# Demo

Use these instructions for the task.
```

**Command**

```console
$ jastr run demo
```

**CLI output** — exit 0

```console
# Demo

Use these instructions for the task.
```

</details>

### RUN-FR-0002 — CLI rejects unknown commands

The CLI rejects commands other than `run`, `generate`, `help`, `--help`, `-h`, `--version`, and `-V`, reporting the expected command shape.

| Criterion | Statement | Coverage |
| --- | --- | --- |
| AC-0001 | An unknown command exits with code 1. | ✅ `unknown-command` |
| AC-0002 | An unknown command prints the stable command-shape Error-prefixed stderr message. | ✅ `unknown-command` |

#### Case: Reject unknown commands

Description: Shows how unknown commands are rejected before any project lookup.

Covers: AC-0001, AC-0002

<details>
<summary>Input, command & output</summary>

**Input project**

_Empty — no `.jastr/` directory present (command ran from the project root)._

**Command**

```console
$ jastr invalid-command
```

**CLI output** — exit 1

```console
Error: Expected command shape: jastr run <template-ref> [input flags...] or jastr generate agent-skill <template-ref> --out <path> [--force].
```

</details>

### RUN-FR-0003 — CLI rejects invalid template references

A template reference must be syntactically either a template id matching `^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]*$` or a `.md` file path; a reference that is neither is rejected by the validator.

| Criterion | Statement | Coverage |
| --- | --- | --- |
| AC-0001 | An invalid template reference exits with code 1. | ✅ `invalid-skill-name` |
| AC-0002 | An invalid template reference prints an Error-prefixed stderr message. | ✅ `invalid-skill-name` |

#### Case: Reject invalid template references

Description: Shows how a syntactically invalid template reference is rejected by the validator.

Covers: AC-0001, AC-0002

<details>
<summary>Input, command & output</summary>

**Input project** — ran from the project root

```text
./
└─ .jastr/
   └─ demo/
      └─ template.md
```

`.jastr/demo/template.md`

```md
---
name: demo
description: Demo skill
---
```

**Command**

```console
$ jastr run INVALID
```

**CLI output** — exit 1

```console
Error: Template reference INVALID must be a template id or a .md file path.
```

</details>

### RUN-FR-0004 — CLI rejects missing project root

A named template reference fails when no `.jastr/` directory is found ascending from the current directory.

| Criterion | Statement | Coverage |
| --- | --- | --- |
| AC-0001 | A missing project root exits with code 1. | ✅ `missing-project-root` |
| AC-0002 | A missing project root prints an Error-prefixed stderr message. | ✅ `missing-project-root` |

#### Case: Reject missing project root

Description: Shows how run command fails when no .jastr directory exists.

Covers: AC-0001, AC-0002

<details>
<summary>Input, command & output</summary>

**Input project**

_Empty — no `.jastr/` directory present (command ran from the project root)._

**Command**

```console
$ jastr run demo
```

**CLI output** — exit 1

```console
Error: No .jastr directory found from the current directory.
```

</details>

### RUN-FR-0005 — CLI rejects missing templates

The CLI fails when the requested template id does not have a template file at `.jastr/<template-id>/template.md`.

| Criterion | Statement | Coverage |
| --- | --- | --- |
| AC-0001 | A missing template exits with code 1. | ✅ `missing-skill` |
| AC-0002 | A missing template prints an Error-prefixed stderr message. | ✅ `missing-skill` |

#### Case: Reject missing template

Description: Shows how requesting a non-existent template fails with a clear error.

Covers: AC-0001, AC-0002

<details>
<summary>Input, command & output</summary>

**Input project** — ran from the project root

```text
./
└─ .jastr/
   └─ demo/
      └─ template.md
```

`.jastr/demo/template.md`

```md
---
name: demo
description: Demo skill
---
```

**Command**

```console
$ jastr run no-such-skill
```

**CLI output** — exit 1

```console
Error: Template no-such-skill was not found at .jastr/no-such-skill/template.md.
```

</details>

### RUN-FR-0006 — Run renders a direct file template

The CLI renders a direct `.md` template path without requiring a `.jastr/` project root.

| Criterion | Statement | Coverage |
| --- | --- | --- |
| AC-0001 | Direct-file run exits with code 0 when the template and inputs are valid. | ✅ `direct-file-run` |
| AC-0002 | Direct-file run prints rendered Markdown to stdout. | ✅ `direct-file-run` |
| AC-0003 | Direct-file run prints nothing to stderr. | ✅ `direct-file-run` |

#### Case: Run a direct file template

Description: Render a .md template path without requiring a .jastr project root.

Covers: AC-0001, AC-0002, AC-0003

<details>
<summary>Input, command & output</summary>

**Input project** — ran from the project root

```text
./
└─ templates/
   └─ direct.md
```

`templates/direct.md`

```md
---
inputs:
  language:
    type: string
    required: true
---
Direct {{language}}
```

**Command**

```console
$ jastr run templates/direct.md --language=typescript
```

**CLI output** — exit 0

```console
Direct typescript
```

</details>

### RUN-FR-0007 — CLI rejects invalid template references

A template reference must be syntactically either a template id or a `.md` file path; the CLI does not use filesystem existence to disambiguate.

| Criterion | Statement | Coverage |
| --- | --- | --- |
| AC-0001 | An invalid template reference exits with code 1. | ✅ `invalid-template-reference` |
| AC-0002 | An invalid template reference prints the stable Error-prefixed message. | ✅ `invalid-template-reference` |

#### Case: Reject an invalid template reference

Description: Template references must be syntactically either a template id or a .md file path.

Covers: AC-0001, AC-0002

<details>
<summary>Input, command & output</summary>

**Input project**

_Empty — no `.jastr/` directory present (command ran from the project root)._

**Command**

```console
$ jastr run BadName
```

**CLI output** — exit 1

```console
Error: Template reference BadName must be a template id or a .md file path.
```

</details>

## Inputs

### INPUT-FR-0001 — String inputs interpolate into rendered output

A declared string input supplied through a CLI flag is available to template interpolation.

| Criterion | Statement | Coverage |
| --- | --- | --- |
| AC-0001 | A supplied string input replaces the matching interpolation placeholder. | ✅ `string-interpolation` |

#### Case: Interpolate a string input

Description: Shows how template placeholders expand declared string inputs.

Covers: AC-0001

<details>
<summary>Input, command & output</summary>

**Input project** — ran from the project root

```text
./
└─ .jastr/
   └─ demo/
      └─ template.md
```

`.jastr/demo/template.md`

```md
---
name: demo
description: Demo skill
inputs:
  target-file:
    type: string
    required: false
---
Analyze `{{target-file}}`.
```

**Command**

```console
$ jastr run demo --target-file=src/index.ts
```

**CLI output** — exit 0

```console
Analyze `src/index.ts`.
```

</details>

### INPUT-FR-0002 — Missing optional inputs are rejected in interpolation

An optional input that is not supplied fails rendering when directly interpolated. Authors must guard optional interpolations with conditionals.

| Criterion | Statement | Coverage |
| --- | --- | --- |
| AC-0001 | Missing optional input interpolation exits with code 1. | ✅ `missing-optional-interpolation` |
| AC-0002 | Missing optional input interpolation prints an Error-prefixed stderr message. | ✅ `missing-optional-interpolation` |

#### Case: Optional input in interpolation

Description: Shows how interpolating a missing optional input fails at run time.

Covers: AC-0001, AC-0002

<details>
<summary>Input, command & output</summary>

**Input project** — ran from the project root

```text
./
└─ .jastr/
   └─ demo/
      └─ template.md
```

`.jastr/demo/template.md`

```md
---
name: demo
description: Demo skill
inputs:
  target-file:
    type: string
    required: false
---
Analyze {{target-file}}.
```

**Command**

```console
$ jastr run demo
```

**CLI output** — exit 1

```console
Error: Input target-file is optional and was not provided for interpolation.
```

</details>

### INPUT-FR-0003 — Missing required inputs are rejected

A template-declared required input must be supplied by the caller.

| Criterion | Statement | Coverage |
| --- | --- | --- |
| AC-0001 | Missing required input exits with code 1. | ✅ `missing-required-input` |
| AC-0002 | Missing required input prints an Error-prefixed stderr message. | ✅ `missing-required-input` |

#### Case: Missing Required Input

Description: Shows Jastr input validation errors.

Covers: AC-0001, AC-0002

<details>
<summary>Input, command & output</summary>

**Input project** — ran from the project root

```text
./
└─ .jastr/
   └─ demo/
      └─ template.md
```

`.jastr/demo/template.md`

```md
---
name: demo
description: Demo skill
```


</details>

### INPUT-FR-0004 — Invalid enum values are rejected

Enum inputs accept only values declared in the template frontmatter.

| Criterion | Statement | Coverage |
| --- | --- | --- |
| AC-0001 | A value outside the enum's allowed set exits with code 1. | ✅ `invalid-enum-value` |
| AC-0002 | A value outside the enum's allowed set prints an Error-prefixed stderr message. | ✅ `invalid-enum-value` |

#### Case: Invalid Enum Value

Description: Shows Jastr input validation errors.

Covers: AC-0001, AC-0002

<details>
<summary>Input, command & output</summary>

**Input project** — ran from the project root

```text
./
└─ .jastr/
   └─ demo/
      └─ template.md
```

`.jastr/demo/template.md`

```md
---
name: demo
description: Demo skill
inputs:
  language:
    type: enum
    values: [typescript, python]
    required: true
---
Selected language: {{language}}
```

**Command**

```console
$ jastr run demo --language=ruby
```

**CLI output** — exit 1

```console
Error: Invalid value ruby for --language. Expected one of: typescript, python.
```

</details>

## Flags

### FLAGS-FR-0001 — Boolean inputs accept supported flag forms

Boolean inputs can be supplied as a bare flag, as `=true`, or as `=false`.

| Criterion | Statement | Coverage |
| --- | --- | --- |
| AC-0001 | A bare boolean flag sets the input to true. | ✅ `boolean-bare` |
| AC-0002 | A boolean flag with `=true` sets the input to true. | ✅ `boolean-true` |
| AC-0003 | A boolean flag with `=false` sets the input to false. | ✅ `boolean-false` |

#### Case: Boolean input (bare)

Description: Shows how boolean inputs render in template output.

Covers: AC-0001

<details>

```text
./

   └─ demo/
      └─ template.md
```

### FLAGS-FR-0004 — Syntactically invalid flags are rejected

The CLI rejects flag tokens that do not match the `--name` or `--name=value` forms, including bare positional words and `--no-` negation.

| Criterion | Statement | Coverage |
| --- | --- | --- |
| AC-0001 | A bare positional word instead of a flag exits with code 1. | ✅ `invalid-flag-bareword` |
| AC-0002 | A bare positional word instead of a flag prints an Error-prefixed stderr message. | ✅ `invalid-flag-bareword` |
| AC-0003 | A `--no-` negation flag exits with code 1. | ✅ `invalid-flag-no-negation` |
| AC-0004 | A `--no-` negation flag prints an Error-prefixed stderr message. | ✅ `invalid-flag-no-negation` |

```console
Error: Boolean negation form --no-dry-run is not supported.
```

</details>

### FLAGS-FR-0005 — Empty input values are rejected

String and enum inputs reject an empty value supplied as `--name=`.

| Criterion | Statement | Coverage |
| --- | --- | --- |
| AC-0001 | An empty input value exits with code 1. | ✅ `flag-empty-value` |
| AC-0002 | An empty input value prints an Error-prefixed stderr message. | ✅ `flag-empty-value` |

#### Case: Reject empty input value

Description: Shows how an empty string input value is rejected.

Covers: AC-0001, AC-0002

<details>
<summary>Input, command & output</summary>

**Input project** — ran from the project root

```text
./
└─ .jastr/
   └─ demo/
      └─ template.md
```

`.jastr/demo/template.md`

```md
---
name: demo
description: Demo skill
inputs:
  target-file:
    type: string
    required: false
---
ok
```

**Command**

```console
$ jastr run demo --target-file=
```

**CLI output** — exit 1

```console
Error: Input --target-file cannot be empty.
```

</details>

### FLAGS-FR-0006 — String and enum inputs require a value

String and enum inputs must be supplied as `--name=value`; a bare `--name` flag is rejected for non-boolean inputs.

| Criterion | Statement | Coverage |
| --- | --- | --- |
| AC-0001 | A bare flag for a string or enum input exits with code 1. | ✅ `flag-requires-value` |
| AC-0002 | A bare flag for a string or enum input prints an Error-prefixed stderr message. | ✅ `flag-requires-value` |

#### Case: Reject bare flag for string input

Description: Shows how a string input supplied as a bare flag is rejected.

Covers: AC-0001, AC-0002

<details>
<summary>Input, command & output</summary>

**Input project** — ran from the project root

```text
./
└─ .jastr/
   └─ demo/
      └─ template.md
```

`.jastr/demo/template.md`

```md
---
name: demo
description: Demo skill
inputs:
  target-file:
    type: string
    required: false
---
ok
```

**Command**

```console
$ jastr run demo --target-file
```

**CLI output** — exit 1

```console
Error: Input --target-file requires --target-file=value.
```

</details>

### FLAGS-FR-0007 — Invalid boolean values are rejected

A boolean input supplied with a value other than `true` or `false` is rejected.

| Criterion | Statement | Coverage |
| --- | --- | --- |
| AC-0001 | A boolean flag with a non-boolean value exits with code 1. | ✅ `flag-invalid-boolean` |
| AC-0002 | A boolean flag with a non-boolean value prints an Error-prefixed stderr message. | ✅ `flag-invalid-boolean` |

#### Case: Reject invalid boolean value

Description: Shows how a boolean input with a non-boolean value is rejected.

Covers: AC-0001, AC-0002

<details>
<summary>Input, command & output</summary>

**Input project** — ran from the project root

```text
./
└─ .jastr/
   └─ demo/
      └─ template.md
```

`.jastr/demo/template.md`

```md
---
name: demo
description: Demo skill
inputs:
  dry-run:
    type: boolean
    required: false
---
ok
```

**Command**

```console
$ jastr run demo --dry-run=yes
```

**CLI output** — exit 1

```console
Error: Boolean input --dry-run must be true, false, or a bare flag.
```

</details>

## Conditions

### COND-FR-0001 — Enum inputs select conditional branches

Template conditionals can branch on enum input values.

| Criterion | Statement | Coverage |
| --- | --- | --- |
| AC-0001 | An enum input selects the matching if or else-if branch. | ✅ `enum-branch` |

#### Case: Select a branch from an enum input

Description: Shows how `run` renders only the branch selected by `--language`.

Covers: AC-0001

<details>
<summary>Input, command & output</summary>

**Input project** — ran from the project root

```text
./
└─ .jastr/
   └─ demo/
      └─ template.md
```

`.jastr/demo/template.md`

```md
---
name: demo
description: Demo skill
inputs:
  language:
    type: enum
    values: [typescript, python]
    required: true
---
::::if{condition="${language} == 'typescript'"}
Use the TypeScript checklist.
::::
::::else-if{condition="${language} == 'python'"}
Use the Python checklist.
::::
```

**Command**

```console
$ jastr run demo --language=typescript
```

**CLI output** — exit 0

```console
Use the TypeScript checklist.
```

</details>

### COND-FR-0002 — Missing optional inputs are false in conditions

An optional input that is not supplied evaluates false in conditional expressions.

| Criterion | Statement | Coverage |
| --- | --- | --- |
| AC-0001 | A missing optional input takes the false branch. | ✅ `missing-optional-condition` |

```md
description: Demo skill

  target-file:
    type: string
    required: false
---
::::if{condition="${target-file}"}
Has target
::::
::::else
No target file.
::::
```


```console
No target file.
```

</details>

### COND-FR-0003 — Boolean inputs are truthy in conditions

A supplied boolean input evaluates to its truth value in conditional expressions, selecting the matching branch.

| Criterion | Statement | Coverage |
| --- | --- | --- |
| AC-0001 | A supplied boolean input selects the true branch. | ✅ `boolean-condition` |
| AC-0002 | A false boolean input selects the else branch. | ✅ `boolean-condition-false` |

```md
Dry run enabled.
::::

Not a dry run.
```

String equality and inequality comparisons in condition expressions select the branch matching the compared value.

| AC-0002 | A string inequality (!=) comparison selects the matching branch. | ✅ `string-inequality` |

```md
TypeScript selected.
```

### COND-FR-0005 — Compound conditions select branches

Conditional expressions support logical operators AND (&&), OR (||), and NOT (!).

| Criterion | Statement | Coverage |
| --- | --- | --- |
| AC-0001 | An AND expression selects the branch when both sides are true. | ✅ `compound-condition-and` |
| AC-0002 | An OR expression selects the branch when at least one side is true. | ✅ `compound-condition-or` |
| AC-0003 | A NOT expression selects the branch when the operand is false. | ✅ `compound-condition-not`, `condition-not-standalone` |
| AC-0004 | Parentheses group a subexpression so it evaluates before surrounding operators. | ✅ `condition-parentheses` |

#### Case: Compound condition with AND

Description: Shows how AND (&&) selects the branch when both sides are true.

Covers: AC-0001

<details>
<summary>Input, command & output</summary>

**Input project** — ran from the project root

```text
./
└─ .jastr/
   └─ demo/
      └─ template.md
```

`.jastr/demo/template.md`

```md
---
name: demo
description: Demo skill
inputs:
  flag-a:
    type: boolean
    required: false
  flag-b:
    type: boolean
    required: false
  flag-c:
    type: boolean
    required: false
---
::::if{condition="${flag-a} && ${flag-b}"}
AND true
::::
::::else-if{condition="${flag-a} || ${flag-b}"}
OR true
::::
::::else-if{condition="!${flag-c}"}
NOT true
::::
```

**Command**

```console
$ jastr run demo --flag-a --flag-b
```

**CLI output** — exit 0

```console
AND true
```

</details>

#### Case: Compound condition with NOT

```md
::::
```

### COND-FR-0006 — Conditional containers can be nested

Directives support nested conditional containers using longer outer fences.

| Criterion | Statement | Coverage |
| --- | --- | --- |
| AC-0001 | A nested conditional selects the correct inner branch. | ✅ `nested-conditions` |

#### Case: Nested conditional containers

Description: Shows how nested conditional directives select the correct inner branch.

Covers: AC-0001

<summary>Input, command & output</summary>

```text
./
└─ .jastr/
   └─ demo/
      └─ template.md
```

```md
name: demo
description: Demo skill
inputs:
  mode:
    type: enum
    values: [full, quick]
    required: true
---
::::if{condition="${mode} == 'full'"}
outer
:::if{condition="${mode} == 'full'"}
inner
:::
::::
```

```console
$ jastr run demo --mode=full
```

**CLI output** — exit 0

```console
outer
inner
```

</details>

### COND-FR-0007 — Equality uses typed semantics

Condition equality compares both runtime type and value, so a boolean input is not equal to a string literal with the same text.

| Criterion | Statement | Coverage |
| --- | --- | --- |
| AC-0001 | A boolean input does not equal a string literal of the same text. | ✅ `typed-equality` |

```text
   └─ demo/
```

```md

name: demo
description: Demo skill
inputs:
  dry-run:
    type: boolean
    required: false
---
::::if{condition="${dry-run} == 'true'"}
String equal.
::::
::::else
Typed not equal.
::::
```

```console
$ jastr run demo --dry-run=true
```

**CLI output** — exit 0

```console
Typed not equal.
```

</details>

| AC-0003 | Absolute include paths are rejected. | ✅ `include-absolute-rejected` |
| AC-0004 | Home-relative (~) include paths are rejected. | ✅ `include-tilde-rejected` |

### INCLUDE-FR-0004 — Include cycles are detected and rejected

Circular includes are detected during template loading and rejected with a clear error message.

| Criterion | Statement | Coverage |
| --- | --- | --- |
| AC-0001 | An include cycle exits with code 1. | ✅ `include-cycle` |
| AC-0002 | An include cycle prints an Error-prefixed stderr message. | ✅ `include-cycle` |

#### Case: Reject include cycles

Description: Shows how circular includes are detected and rejected.

Covers: AC-0001, AC-0002

<details>

```text
└─ .jastr/
   └─ demo/
      ├─ a.md
      ├─ b.md
      └─ template.md
```

`.jastr/demo/a.md`

```md
A
::include{path="b.md"}
```

`.jastr/demo/b.md`

```md
B
::include{path="a.md"}
```

`.jastr/demo/template.md`

```md
---
name: demo
description: Demo skill
---
Root
::include{path="a.md"}
```

**Command**

```console
$ jastr run demo
```

**CLI output** — exit 1

```console
Error: Include cycle detected: .jastr/demo/a.md -> .jastr/demo/b.md -> .jastr/demo/a.md.
```

</details>

Include directives that reference a nonexistent file are rejected.

| Criterion | Statement | Coverage |
| --- | --- | --- |
| AC-0001 | A missing include file exits with code 1. | ✅ `include-not-found` |
| AC-0002 | A missing include file prints an Error-prefixed stderr message. | ✅ `include-not-found` |

### INCLUDE-FR-0006 — Include read errors are reported

An include path that resolves inside the project root but cannot be read as a file (for example, it points to a directory) fails with a normalized filesystem cause rather than the raw platform error.

| Criterion | Statement | Coverage |
| --- | --- | --- |
| AC-0001 | An include path that points to a directory exits with code 1. | ✅ `include-read-error` |
| AC-0002 | An include path that points to a directory prints an Error-prefixed stderr message naming the filesystem cause. | ✅ `include-read-error` |

#### Case: Report include read errors

Description: Shows how an include path pointing to a directory fails with a normalized cause.

Covers: AC-0001, AC-0002

<details>
<summary>Input, command & output</summary>

**Input project** — ran from the project root

```text

└─ .jastr/
   └─ demo/
      ├─ subdir/
      │  └─ keep.md
      └─ template.md
```

`.jastr/demo/subdir/keep.md`

```md
placeholder so the directory exists in git
```

`.jastr/demo/template.md`

```md
---
name: demo
description: Demo skill
---
::include{path="subdir"}
```

**Command**

```console
$ jastr run demo
```

**CLI output** — exit 1

```console
Error: Include file subdir could not be read: EISDIR.
```

</details>

### INCLUDE-FR-0007 — Direct-file includes stay inside the template directory

Direct-file template includes resolve relative to the including file and cannot escape the initial direct template file directory.

| Criterion | Statement | Coverage |
| --- | --- | --- |
| AC-0001 | A direct-file include escaping the template directory exits with code 1. | ✅ `direct-file-include-escape` |
| AC-0002 | A direct-file include escaping the template directory prints the stable Error-prefixed message. | ✅ `direct-file-include-escape` |

#### Case: Reject a direct-file include escaping the template directory

Description: Direct-file includes are contained to the direct template file directory.

Covers: AC-0001, AC-0002

<details>
<summary>Input, command & output</summary>

**Input project** — ran from the project root

```text
./
├─ outside.md
└─ templates/
   └─ direct.md
```

`outside.md`

```md
outside
```

`templates/direct.md`

```md
::include{path="../outside.md"}
```

**Command**

```console
$ jastr run templates/direct.md
```

**CLI output** — exit 1

```console
Error: Include path ../outside.md escapes the template directory.
```

</details>

## Generate

### GEN-FR-0001 — Generate agent-skill writes a wrapper skill

The `generate agent-skill` command writes a minimal Agent Skill wrapper to an explicit output path from a template's `targets.agent-skill` metadata.

| Criterion | Statement | Coverage |
| --- | --- | --- |
| AC-0001 | Generate exits with code 0 for a valid template and output path. | ✅ `generate-router` |
| AC-0002 | Generate writes the expected Agent Skill wrapper file. | ✅ `generate-router` |
| AC-0003 | Generate prints the generated path and source template path. | ✅ `generate-router` |
| AC-0004 | Generate creates missing parent directories for the output path. | ✅ `generate-router` |

#### Case: Generate a router skill

Description: Shows how generate writes a minimal router skill from a template, creating the missing out/ parent directory.

Covers: AC-0001, AC-0002, AC-0003, AC-0004

<details>
<summary>Input, command & output</summary>

**Input project** — ran from the project root

```text
./
└─ .jastr/
   └─ demo/
      └─ template.md
```

`.jastr/demo/template.md`

```md
---
targets:
  agent-skill:
    name: demo
    description: Demo skill
---
Hello
```

**Command**

```console
$ jastr generate agent-skill demo --out out/SKILL.md
```

**Output files**

`out/SKILL.md`

````md
---
name: demo
description: Demo skill
---

Run this command and follow its output exactly:

```bash
jastr run demo
```

If the command exits non-zero, report the exact error output to the user and stop.
````

**CLI output** — exit 0

```console
Generated `out/SKILL.md` from template `.jastr/demo/template.md`
```

</details>

The `generate agent-skill` command requires callers to provide `--out`.

| AC-0002 | Missing `--out` prints an Error-prefixed stderr message. | ✅ `generate-missing-out` |

| --- | --- | --- |
| AC-0001 | An invalid Agent Skill name exits with code 1. | ✅ `generate-invalid-name` |
| AC-0002 | An invalid Agent Skill name prints an Error-prefixed stderr message. | ✅ `generate-invalid-name` |

### GEN-FR-0007 — Generate passes through target frontmatter and omits owned fields

The generated Agent Skill wrapper frontmatter renders `targets.agent-skill.name` and `targets.agent-skill.description`, preserves any additional kebab-case `targets.agent-skill.frontmatter` fields, and omits Jastr-owned fields such as `inputs`.

| Criterion | Statement | Coverage |
| --- | --- | --- |
| AC-0001 | Generate writes the expected wrapper file for a template with extra frontmatter. | ✅ `generate-passthrough` |
| AC-0002 | The generated wrapper omits Jastr-owned fields such as inputs. | ✅ `generate-passthrough` |
| AC-0003 | The generated wrapper preserves the name, description, and extension frontmatter fields. | ✅ `generate-passthrough` |

#### Case: Generate passes through frontmatter

Description: Shows how generate preserves author frontmatter fields and omits Jastr-owned inputs.

Covers: AC-0001, AC-0002, AC-0003

<details>
<summary>Input, command & output</summary>

**Input project** — ran from the project root

```text
./
└─ .jastr/
   └─ demo/
      └─ template.md
```

`.jastr/demo/template.md`

```md
---
targets:
  agent-skill:
    name: demo
    description: A demo skill
    frontmatter:
      license: MIT
      my-extension-field: custom-value
inputs:
  language:
    type: enum
    values: [typescript, python]
    required: true
---
::::if{condition="${language} == 'typescript'"}
TS
::::
```

**Command**

```console
$ jastr generate agent-skill demo --out=out/SKILL.md
```

**Output files**

`out/SKILL.md`

````md
---
name: demo
description: A demo skill
license: MIT
my-extension-field: custom-value
---

Run this command and follow its output exactly:

```bash
jastr run demo $ARGUMENTS
```

If the command exits non-zero, report the exact error output to the user and stop.
````

**CLI output** — exit 0

```console
Generated `out/SKILL.md` from template `.jastr/demo/template.md`
```

</details>

### GEN-FR-0010 — Generate rejects unsupported targets

`agent-skill` is the only implemented generate target; other targets remain unavailable until their own specs are implemented.

| Criterion | Statement | Coverage |

| AC-0001 | An unsupported generate target exits with code 1. | ✅ `unsupported-generate-target` |

```console

```

### GEN-FR-0011 — Generate forwards $ARGUMENTS only when the template declares inputs

The generated Agent Skill wrapper's `jastr run` command forwards `$ARGUMENTS` only when the source template declares one or more inputs. When the template declares no inputs, the wrapper runs `jastr run <template-ref>` directly with no `$ARGUMENTS`, so the agent does not forward arguments a no-input template would reject.

| Criterion | Statement | Coverage |
| --- | --- | --- |
| AC-0001 | A template that declares no inputs generates a wrapper whose command omits $ARGUMENTS. | ✅ `generate-router` |
| AC-0002 | A template that declares one or more inputs generates a wrapper whose command forwards $ARGUMENTS. | ✅ `generate-passthrough` |

#### Case: Generate passes through frontmatter

Description: Shows how generate preserves author frontmatter fields and omits Jastr-owned inputs.

Covers: AC-0002

```text
./
└─ .jastr/
   └─ demo/
      └─ template.md
```

`.jastr/demo/template.md`

```md
---
targets:
  agent-skill:
    name: demo
    description: A demo skill
    frontmatter:
      license: MIT
      my-extension-field: custom-value
inputs:
  language:
    type: enum
    values: [typescript, python]
    required: true
---
::::if{condition="${language} == 'typescript'"}
TS
::::
```

**Command**

```console
$ jastr generate agent-skill demo --out=out/SKILL.md
```

**Output files**

`out/SKILL.md`

````md
---
name: demo
description: A demo skill
license: MIT
my-extension-field: custom-value
---

Run this command and follow its output exactly:

```bash
jastr run demo $ARGUMENTS
```

If the command exits non-zero, report the exact error output to the user and stop.
````

**CLI output** — exit 0

```console
Generated `out/SKILL.md` from template `.jastr/demo/template.md`
```

</details>

## Output

### OUTPUT-FR-0001 — Uniform error and success output contract

Every failure prints a single-line `Error: <message>` to stderr, exits with code 1, and leaves stdout empty so callers can treat stdout as the rendered instruction channel. Help and version output exit with code 0.

| Criterion | Statement | Coverage |
| --- | --- | --- |
| AC-0001 | A failure prints a single-line Error-prefixed message to stderr. | ✅ `missing-skill` |
| AC-0002 | A failure exits with code 1 and prints nothing to stdout. | ✅ `missing-skill` |
| AC-0003 | Help output exits with code 0. | ✅ `help-root` |
| AC-0004 | Version output exits with code 0. | ✅ `version` |

## Templates

### TEMPLATE-FR-0001 — Templates with malformed frontmatter are rejected

Frontmatter is optional, but when a template opens a `---` frontmatter fence it must close it with `---` on its own line; an unterminated fence is rejected.

| Criterion | Statement | Coverage |
| --- | --- | --- |
| AC-0001 | A template with an unterminated frontmatter fence exits with code 1. | ✅ `invalid-frontmatter` |
| AC-0002 | A template with an unterminated frontmatter fence prints an Error-prefixed stderr message. | ✅ `invalid-frontmatter` |

#### Case: Reject malformed frontmatter

Description: Shows how a template whose frontmatter fence is never closed is rejected.

Covers: AC-0001, AC-0002

<details>
<summary>Input, command & output</summary>

**Input project** — ran from the project root

```text
./
└─ .jastr/
   └─ demo/
      └─ template.md
```

`.jastr/demo/template.md`

```md
---
inputs:
```

**Command**

```console
$ jastr run demo
```

**CLI output** — exit 1

```console
Error: Template frontmatter must close with --- on its own line.
```

</details>

### TEMPLATE-FR-0002 — Inputs must declare requiredness explicitly

Every declared input must set `required: true` or `required: false`; there is no implicit default.

| Criterion | Statement | Coverage |
| --- | --- | --- |
| AC-0001 | An input without an explicit required field exits with code 1. | ✅ `schema-missing-required` |
| AC-0002 | An input without an explicit required field prints an Error-prefixed stderr message. | ✅ `schema-missing-required` |

#### Case: Reject inputs without explicit requiredness

Description: Shows how an input that omits required is rejected.

Covers: AC-0001, AC-0002

<details>
<summary>Input, command & output</summary>

**Input project** — ran from the project root

```text
./
└─ .jastr/
   └─ demo/
      └─ template.md
```

`.jastr/demo/template.md`

```md
---
name: demo
description: Demo skill
inputs:
  target-file:
    type: string
---
Hello
```

**Command**

```console
$ jastr run demo
```

**CLI output** — exit 1

```console
Error: Input target-file must explicitly declare required: true or required: false.
```

</details>

### TEMPLATE-FR-0003 — Unsupported input types are rejected

Input definitions accept only the `string`, `boolean`, and `enum` types.

| Criterion | Statement | Coverage |
| --- | --- | --- |
| AC-0001 | An input with an unsupported type exits with code 1. | ✅ `schema-unsupported-type` |
| AC-0002 | An input with an unsupported type prints an Error-prefixed stderr message. | ✅ `schema-unsupported-type` |

#### Case: Reject unsupported input types

Description: Shows how an input with an unsupported type is rejected.

Covers: AC-0001, AC-0002

<details>
<summary>Input, command & output</summary>

**Input project** — ran from the project root

```text
./
└─ .jastr/
   └─ demo/
      └─ template.md
```

`.jastr/demo/template.md`

```md
---
name: demo
description: Demo skill
inputs:
  count:
    type: number
    required: true
---
Hello
```

**Command**

```console
$ jastr run demo
```

**CLI output** — exit 1

```console
Error: Input count uses unsupported type number.
```

</details>

### TEMPLATE-FR-0004 — Enum inputs must declare values

An enum input must provide a non-empty `values` array.

| Criterion | Statement | Coverage |
| --- | --- | --- |
| AC-0001 | An enum input without values exits with code 1. | ✅ `schema-empty-enum` |
| AC-0002 | An enum input without values prints an Error-prefixed stderr message. | ✅ `schema-empty-enum` |

#### Case: Reject enum inputs without values

Description: Shows how an enum input with no values is rejected.

Covers: AC-0001, AC-0002

<details>
<summary>Input, command & output</summary>

**Input project** — ran from the project root

```text
./
└─ .jastr/
   └─ demo/
      └─ template.md
```

`.jastr/demo/template.md`

```md
---
name: demo
description: Demo skill
inputs:
  choice:
    type: enum
    values: []
    required: true
---
Hello
```

**Command**

```console
$ jastr run demo
```

**CLI output** — exit 1

```console
Error: Enum input choice must declare at least one value.
```

</details>

### TEMPLATE-FR-0005 — Equal-length nested fences are rejected

A nested conditional container must use a shorter colon fence than its outer container; equal-length fences are rejected so the inner close cannot end the outer directive early.

| Criterion | Statement | Coverage |
| --- | --- | --- |
| AC-0001 | An equal-length nested conditional fence exits with code 1. | ✅ `nesting-equal-fence` |
| AC-0002 | An equal-length nested conditional fence prints an Error-prefixed stderr message. | ✅ `nesting-equal-fence` |

#### Case: Reject equal-length nested fences

Description: Shows how a nested conditional with an equal-length fence is rejected.

Covers: AC-0001, AC-0002

<details>
<summary>Input, command & output</summary>

**Input project** — ran from the project root

```text
./
└─ .jastr/
   └─ demo/
      └─ template.md
```

`.jastr/demo/template.md`

```md
---
name: demo
description: Demo skill
inputs:
  mode:
    type: enum
    values: [full, quick]
    required: true
---
:::if{condition="${mode} == 'full'"}
outer
:::if{condition="${mode} == 'full'"}
inner
:::
:::
```

**Command**

```console
$ jastr run demo --mode=full
```

**CLI output** — exit 1

```console
Error: Nested conditional containers require a longer outer fence than inner fences.
```

</details>

### TEMPLATE-FR-0006 — Branch directives must follow an open conditional group

Non-blank content between branches ends the conditional group, so a later `else-if` or `else` is a template error.

| Criterion | Statement | Coverage |
| --- | --- | --- |
| AC-0001 | A branch directive after interrupting content exits with code 1. | ✅ `branch-adjacency` |
| AC-0002 | A branch directive after interrupting content prints an Error-prefixed stderr message. | ✅ `branch-adjacency` |

#### Case: Reject branch directives after interrupting content

Description: Shows how non-blank content between branches ends the group and makes a later else-if an error.

Covers: AC-0001, AC-0002

<details>
<summary>Input, command & output</summary>

**Input project** — ran from the project root

```text
./
└─ .jastr/
   └─ demo/
      └─ template.md
```

`.jastr/demo/template.md`

```md
---
name: demo
description: Demo skill
inputs:
  x:
    type: boolean
    required: false
---
::::if{condition="${x}"}
a
::::
interrupting text
::::else-if{condition="${x}"}
b
::::
```

**Command**

```console
$ jastr run demo
```

**CLI output** — exit 1

```console
Error: else-if directive must immediately follow an if or else-if branch.
```

</details>

### TEMPLATE-FR-0007 — Condition parse errors are rejected

A condition expression that is not valid Jastr condition syntax, such as a bare identifier instead of a `${input-name}` reference, is rejected.

| Criterion | Statement | Coverage |
| --- | --- | --- |
| AC-0001 | A condition parse error exits with code 1. | ✅ `condition-parse-error` |
| AC-0002 | A condition parse error prints an Error-prefixed stderr message. | ✅ `condition-parse-error` |

#### Case: Reject condition parse errors

Description: Shows how a bare identifier instead of a ${input-name} reference is rejected.

Covers: AC-0001, AC-0002

<details>
<summary>Input, command & output</summary>

**Input project** — ran from the project root

```text
./
└─ .jastr/
   └─ demo/
      └─ template.md
```

`.jastr/demo/template.md`

```md
---
name: demo
description: Demo skill
---
::::if{condition="dryrun"}
x
::::
```

**Command**

```console
$ jastr run demo
```

**CLI output** — exit 1

```console
Error: Expected ${input-name} reference.
```

</details>

### TEMPLATE-FR-0008 — Conditions referencing undeclared inputs are rejected

A condition may only reference inputs declared in the template frontmatter.

| Criterion | Statement | Coverage |
| --- | --- | --- |
| AC-0001 | A condition referencing an undeclared input exits with code 1. | ✅ `undeclared-condition-input` |
| AC-0002 | A condition referencing an undeclared input prints an Error-prefixed stderr message. | ✅ `undeclared-condition-input` |

#### Case: Reject conditions referencing undeclared inputs

Description: Shows how a condition that references an undeclared input is rejected.

Covers: AC-0001, AC-0002

<details>
<summary>Input, command & output</summary>

**Input project** — ran from the project root

```text
./
└─ .jastr/
   └─ demo/
      └─ template.md
```

`.jastr/demo/template.md`

```md
---
name: demo
description: Demo skill
---
::::if{condition="${nope}"}
x
::::
```


</details>

### TEMPLATE-FR-0009 — Interpolation referencing undeclared inputs is rejected

A `{{input-name}}` placeholder may only reference inputs declared in the template frontmatter.

| Criterion | Statement | Coverage |
| --- | --- | --- |
| AC-0001 | An interpolation referencing an undeclared input exits with code 1. | ✅ `undeclared-interpolation` |
| AC-0002 | An interpolation referencing an undeclared input prints an Error-prefixed stderr message. | ✅ `undeclared-interpolation` |

#### Case: Reject interpolation referencing undeclared inputs

Description: Shows how a {{input}} placeholder referencing an undeclared input is rejected.

Covers: AC-0001, AC-0002

```text
./
└─ .jastr/
   └─ demo/
      └─ template.md
```
